title: Footwear with dynamic arch system
abstract: The present invention is footwear having a convex shaped outsole with opposing wedge shaped configurations in the bottom of the front sole section and the back sole section which provide rotation of the front sole section and the back sole section in opposite directions when weight is applied. The present invention is also footwear convex shaped in the longitudinal direction with a split sole having opposing wedge shaped configurations in the bottom of the front sole section and the back sole section that provide rotation of the front sole section and the back sole section in opposite directions when weight is applied. The invention further includes footwear having at least one pair of wedges on the outsole which provide footwear having improved arch support. The invention is also footwear with a flexible, elastic, member between the front sole section and the back sole section of the sole.
claims:
Claims We claim: 1. An article of footwear comprising an upper and an elastic sole structure secured to the upper having a front at the toe area and a hack at the heel area, a medial side and a lateral side, a longitudinal length from the front to the back and a transverse width from the medial side to the lateral side, the sole structure comprising: an outsole comprising an underside for contact with the ground having a generally convex shape along the longitudinal length of said footwear when in a non-weight bearing position; said outsole comprising a front end section and a back end section, said front end section located along the entire front sole region of said sole with a connecting portion in the front of the midfoot sole region, said back end section located along the entire rear sole region of said sole with a connecting portion in the back of the midfoot sole region; said underside of said front end section of said outsole curved upward toward the upper surface of said outsole from a place of contact located furthest away from said upper surface to the front of said outsole, said place of contact located in the midfoot sole region; said underside of said back end section of said outsole curved upward toward the upper surface of said outsole from a place of contact located furthest away from said upper surface to the back of said outsole, said place of contact located in the midfoot sole region, said underside of said outsole further comprising a middle section between said places of contact, said underside of said middle section raised above said places of contact; a metal strip connecting said front end section and said back end section, said metal strip extending across the middle section; and 

 wherein when said footwear is worn and weight is placed down onto said outsole, said front end section and said back end section of said outsole each bend about said places of contact and rotate in opposite directions causing the underside of said outsole to flatten in the forefoot sole region and the rear sole region. 2. The footwear according to claim 1, wherein each of said front end section and said back end section are wedge shaped. 3. The footwear according to claim 1, wherein said metal strip is removable and replaceable with a metal strip having different elasticity. 4. The footwear according to claim 1, wherein said places of contact are spaced apart a distance of about 1/3 of the total longitudinal length of said footwear. 5. The footwear according to claim I, wherein said places of contact are spaced apart a distance of less than 1/3 of the total longitudinal length of said footwear, 6. The footwear according to claim 1, wherein said front end section and back end section further comprise magnets having similar polarity, wherein said magnets repel each other when the section move closer together. 7. An adhesive pad for the underside of a foot having a front at a toe area and a back at a heel area, a medial side and a lateral side, a longitudinal length from the front to the back and a transverse width from the medial side to the lateral side, the adhesive pad comprising: an upper surface removably attachable to a sole of a user's foot, said upper surface comprising an adhesive; a bottom surface for contact with the inside of footwear or the ground; a front end section and a back end section, said front end section being the portion of the adhesive pad located in the front sole region of said adhesive pad with a connecting portion in 

 the front of the midfoot sole region, said back end section being the portion of the adhesive pad located in the entire rear sole region of said adhesive pad with a connecting portion in the back of the midfoot sole region; a middle section being the portion of the adhesive pad between the front end section and the back end section; said bottom surface of said back end section sloped upward toward the upper surface of said adhesive pad along the longitudinal length from a place of contact located on a portion of the bottom surface of said back end section closest to the front of said adhesive pad; said bottom surface of said front end section sloped upward toward the upper surface of said adhesive pad along the longitudinal length from a place of contact located on a portion of the bottom surface of said front end section closest to the back of said adhesive pad; said middle section of said adhesive pad raised above said places of contact, and wherein when said adhesive pad is worn and weight is placed down onto said adhesive pad, said front end section and said back end section of said adhesive pad each bend about said places of contact and rotate in opposite directions. 8. The adhesive pad according to claim 7, wherein said places of contact are spaced apart a distance of about 1/3 of the total longitudinal length of said adhesive pad. 9. The adhesive pad according to claim 7, wherein said places of contact are spaced apart a distance of less than 1/3 of the total longitudinal length of said adhesive pad. 10. The adhesive pad according to claim 7, wherein said front end section is configured to attach to the underside of a user's big toe without any portion of the adhesive pad attaching to other toes. 11. An adhesive pad for the underside of a foot having a front at a toe area and a back at a heel area, a medial side and a lateral side, a longitudinal length from the front to the back and a transverse width from the medial side to the lateral side, the adhesive pad comprising: an upper surface removably attachable to a sole of a user's foot, said upper surface comprising an adhesive; a bottom surface for contact with the inside of footwear or the ground; a front end section and a back end section, said front end section being the portion of the adhesive pad located in the front sole region of said adhesive pad with a connecting portion in the front of the midfoot sole region, said back end section being the portion of the adhesive pad located in the entire rear sole region of said adhesive pad with a connecting portion in the back of the midfoot sole region; a middle section being the portion of the adhesive pad between the front end section and the back end section; said bottom surface of said back end section sloped upward toward the upper surface of said adhesive pad along the longitudinal length from a) a place of contact located on a portion of the bottom surface of said back end section closest to the front of said adhesive pad to b) the back; a first portion of said bottom surface of said front end section on the medial side of said adhesive pad sloped upward toward the upper surface of said adhesive pad along the longitudinal length from a) a place of contact located on a portion of the bottom surface of said front end section closest to the back of said adhesive pad and only on the medial side of the bottom b) the front; said middle section of said adhesive pad raised above said places of contact, and 

 wherein when said adhesive pad is worn and weight is placed down onto said adhesive pad, said front end section and said back end section of said adhesive pad each bend about said places of contact and rotate in opposite directions, 12. The adhesive pad according to claim 11, wherein said places of contact are spaced apart a distance of about 1/3 of the total longitudinal length of said adhesive pad. 13. The adhesive pad according to claim 11, wherein said places of contact are spaced apart a distance of less than 1/3 of the total longitudinal length of said adhesive pad. 14. The adhesive pad according to claim 1 1 , wherein said front end section is configured to attach to the underside of a user's big toe without any portion of the adhesive pad attaching to other toes. 15. The adhesive pad according to claim 11, further comprising a second portion of said bottom surface of said front end section on the lateral side of said bottom sloped upward toward the upper surface of said adhesive pad along the longitudinal length from a) a place of contact located on a portion of the bottom surface of said front end section closest to the back of said adhesive pad and only on the lateral side of said bottom to b) the front; wherein the bottom surface of said front end section between said first portion and said second portion of said bottom surface is raised above said places of contact. 16. An adhesive pad for the underside of a foot having a front at a toe area and a back at a heel area, a medial side and a lateral side, a longitudinal length from the front to the back and a transverse width from the medial side to the lateral side, the adhesive pad comprising: an upper surface removably attachable to a sole of a user's foot, said upper surface comprising an adhesive; a bottom surface for contact with the inside of footwear or the ground; 

 a first portion of said bottom surface located in a front end section and medial to a center of the adhesive pad, said first portion sloped upward toward the upper surface along the traverse width from a) a place of contact located on the bottom surface to the medial side of said adhesive pad to b) an edge on the medial side; and a second portion of said bottom surface located in the front end section and lateral to a center of the adhesive pad, said second portion sloped upward toward the upper surface along the traverse width from a) a place of contact located on the bottom surface to the lateral side of said adhesive pad to b) an edge on the lateral side; wherein the bottom surface between said first portion and said second portion of said bottom surface is raised above said places of contact; wherein when said adhesive pad is worn and weight is placed down onto said adhesive pad, said medial side and said lateral side of said front end section each bend about said places of contact and rotate in opposite directions. 17. The adhesive pad according to claim 16, configured to attach to the underside of a user's big toe without any portion of the adhesive pad attaching to other toes. 18. The adhesive pad according to claim 17, further comprising a bottom surface in a back end section sloped upward toward the upper surface along the longitudinal length from a) a place of contact to b) the back of said adhesive pad. 19. The adhesive pad according to claim 18, wherein said first portion of said bottom surface of said front end section is also sloped upward along the longitudinal length from a) the back most part of the first portion to b) the front most part of the first portion; and 

 wherein said second portion of said bottom surface of said front end section is also sloped upward along the longitudinal length from a) the back most part of the second portion to b) the front most part of the second portion.
